name: Oriental Meatballs
recipeInstructions:
1. Place ground meat, eggs, garlic, and spices in a bowl.
2. Wet the slices of bread with water and add to bowl.
3. Mix everything together until well combined.
4. Shape into 1-inch balls and roll in flour.
5. Heat about 1/4 cup vegetable oil in a skillet over medium heat.
6. Add meatballs a few at a time, browning them lightly without cooking thoroughly.
7. If needed, add more oil to keep the flour from absorbing too much grease.
8. Remove browned meatballs and set aside.
9. Clean the skillet, leaving crispy pieces behind.
10. Stir in sugar, vinegar, soy sauce, and water (or wine) until heated through.
11. Return meatballs to the skillet; cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the meatballs are glazed.
12. Taste and adjust seasoning with more sugar if necessary.
13. Serve hot over cooked white rice.
Notes:
This recipe originally called for ground turkey but was switched to beef after a suspicious ingredient was found. Ground turkey or a mix of turkey and beef can be used instead. The dish improves with age; it can be made ahead, stored frozen, or refrigerated and reheated later, which may leave a layer of grease on top.
